snapshot.scope: Manual review binds public/documentation surfaces, control manifests, verifier code and shared executable schemas; automated gates execute current implementation evidence separately.

reviewers.task: Approved HTML-equivalent production UI and native window shell review
reviewers.independence: UI Design accepted the exact combined Development hash after directly owning production renderer bytes. Planning reproduced the 18-path patch/content identities, empty index and diff-check, then reviewed owner separation, native-shell mapping, visual evidence layers and unchanged status boundary without editing frozen paths.

dimensions.summary: Existing UI, Technical Architecture, Quality and AI-development owners locate the sole HTML baseline, production renderer ownership, window-shell mapping and evidence workflow.
Owners agree on white canvas, #202322 text, one 58px titlebar, macOS hiddenInset, Windows transparent native overlay, Linux defaults, and no renderer redesign or frame:false.
Functional window-shell evidence is distinct from macOS real-Electron visual review and still-open Windows visual proof; semantic lock v3 has 1,212 claims and no status changes.
UI Design owns production renderer bytes and final visual review; Development owns typed interfaces, integration and platform evidence, returning bounded interface gaps instead of redrawing UI.
No document or decision is added; existing owner and trace text is compressed while fixed leanness budgets remain green.

verification.evidence: The exact frozen combined candidate passes docs, decisions, UI visual, independent review, TRC-001 through TRC-005, CON-001 through CON-004, dependencies, fixtures, boundaries, no telemetry, source artifacts, LEN-001 through LEN-005 and all quality dimensions.

verification.command: Exact candidate functional and visual evidence
verification.evidence: Focused 7 files/104 tests, typecheck, build and development launch pass. Real macOS Electron at 420x760 shows one integrated 58px renderer titlebar with native traffic lights and no duplicate row, clipping or overlap; UI Design accepted the exact final hash.

verification.command: npm run verify:ui-visual && npm run verify:traceability && npm run verify:contracts
verification.evidence: The approved canvas/text tokens and structural contract pass; semantic lock v3/1,212 and CON-001 through CON-004 pass with Requirement, Exit and Phase states unchanged.
LEN-001 through LEN-005 pass within the fixed entry, trace and governed Markdown budgets.

coordination.taskTitle: Approved production UI equivalence and native window shell
coordination.compatibility: The candidate changes production renderer presentation and main-owned window options without new IPC, durable schema, dependency, provider, permission or backend capability. Existing typed behavior and unavailable-state truth are preserved.
coordination.acknowledgement: Development froze exact f2cc966e/141adc36 bytes after UI Design directly owned the renderer replacement and accepted the combined candidate. Planning bound existing owners, visual contract, acceptance, semantic lock and this recipe without changing those bytes.

nonBlockingRisks: Real packaged Windows visual evidence must prove retained caption controls, safe area, no duplicate titlebar and stable transparency; only the approved main-owned #f7f7f7 fallback is allowed.
The authoritative complete test matrix and cross-platform package/distribution gates remain the post-bind PR CI node; local contention produced two unrelated timeout-only default runs.
Unavailable capabilities remain polished local development states, not executable feature or status evidence.
